expected_intents: recover access when MFA device is lost
change email or password while logged in
revoke a session / sign out other devices
critical_paths: sign up -> verify -> first authenticated action
forgot password -> reset link -> new password -> log in
log in -> access protected resource -> log out -> resource no longer reachable
common_failure_modes: error messages that reveal whether an account exists (enumeration)
reset link that does not expire or is reusable
session not invalidated on logout or password change
MFA bypassable via an alternate path
generic 'something went wrong' with no recovery route
password rules enforced silently (input cleared, no message)
often_missing_paths: recover access when the MFA device is lost
delete the account and associated data
evidence_cautions: do not brute-force or lock real accounts; blocked -> evidence debt
do not send real reset emails to real inboxes; avoided -> evidence debt
